docs.title: MultiSpec
docs.text: Multi-domain specification orchestration for humans and AI agents.

docs.title: What is MultiSpec?
docs.text:
MultiSpec bridges the gap between organizational intent (MRD, PRD, UXD) and executable specifications for AI coding agents. It provides a structured workflow for:

  • Domain-specific authoring - Separate specs for PM, UX, Engineering
  • GTM synthesis - LLM-generated press releases, FAQs, narratives (Working Backwards)
  • Technical synthesis - LLM-generated TRD, IRD from source specs
  • Structured evaluation - Per-domain LLM judges with customizable rubrics
  • Reconciliation - Conflict detection and tradeoff resolution
  • Target adapters - Export to SpecKit, GSD, GasTown, GasCity, OpenSpec

docs.title: Document Lifecycle
docs.text:
HUMAN-AUTHORED (Source)
  mrd.md → prd.md → uxd.md
           ↓
LLM-GENERATED (GTM) ← Working Backwards
  press.md → faq.md → narrative.md
           ↓
LLM-GENERATED (Technical)
  trd.md → ird.md
           ↓
RECONCILIATION
  All approved specs → spec.md
           ↓
TARGET EXPORT
  spec.md → SpecKit | GSD | GasTown | GasCity | OpenSpec
           ↓
POST-SHIP ALIGNMENT
  spec.md + reality → current-truth.md

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Install
go install github.com/plexusone/multispec/cmd/multispec@v0.4.0

# Initialize a new project
multispec init user-onboarding

# Initialize with a profile (startup, growth, enterprise)
multispec init my-feature --profile startup

# Validate project structure
multispec lint

# Check project status
multispec status

docs.title: Readiness Gates
docs.text:
MultiSpec tracks project readiness through configurable gates:

Gate Description Required specs present All required source specs exist Evaluations passing No blocking evaluation findings Approvals obtained All required specs have approvals Execution spec generated spec.md has been created

docs.title: Multiple Output Formats
docs.text:
Status reports can be generated in multiple formats for different use cases:

  • Text - Terminal output with icons
  • JSON - Programmatic access
  • HTML - Browser-viewable reports with traffic light indicators
  • Markdown - For embedding in documentation

docs.title: MCP Integration
docs.text: MultiSpec includes an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for integration with AI coding assistants like Claude Code and Kiro CLI.

docs.title: Configuration Profiles
docs.text:
Profiles define which specs are required for different product lifecycle stages:

Profile Required Specs Use Case 0-1 hypothesis Idea validation phase startup prd Pre-PMF startups growth prd, uxd, faq 1-N scaling phase enterprise mrd, prd, uxd, trd, press, faq, spec Post-PMF enterprises 
# List available profiles
multispec profiles list

# Initialize with a profile
multispec init my-project --profile startup

# Export a profile for customization
multispec profiles export enterprise ./my-profile

# Use a custom profile
multispec init my-project --profile-dir ./my-profile

Organizations can create custom profiles with their own templates and rubrics. See the Custom Profiles Guide for details.

docs.text:
See the ROADMAP for detailed implementation status and Release Notes for the latest release.

docs.title: Context Sources
docs.text: Context sources enable multispec to gather information from your existing codebases, requirement graphs, and external tools to ground spec synthesis in reality.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
When synthesizing technical specs (TRD, IRD), multispec can automatically gather context from:

  • Git Repositories - Code structure, dependencies, API schemas
  • Graphize Graphs - Requirement traceability data
  • MCP Servers - External tools like Jira, Confluence, Google Docs
  • Local Files - Architecture docs, design decisions

This context is included in LLM prompts to ensure generated specs reflect your actual codebase and organizational knowledge.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Add context sources to your multispec.yaml:

name: my-feature
context:
  repositories:
    - path: /path/to/backend
      graphize: auto
    - url: https://github.com/org/frontend.git
      branch: main
      sparse:
        - src/components
        - src/api

  graphize:
    - path: /path/to/requirements/.graphize
      name: product-requirements

  files:
    - path: docs/architecture.md
      type: architecture
    - path: docs/adr/
      type: decisions

  mcp_servers:
    jira:
      command: npx
      args: ["-y", "@anthropic/jira-mcp"]
      config:
        project: PROJ
        jql: "status != Done"

    confluence:
      command: npx
      args: ["-y", "@anthropic/confluence-mcp"]
      config:
        space: ENGINEERING

  cache_ttl: 5m

docs.title: Local Repository
docs.text:
context:
  repositories:
    - path: /absolute/path/to/repo
      analyze:
        - structure      # Directory tree
        - dependencies   # go.mod, package.json, etc.
        - api_schemas    # OpenAPI, GraphQL, Proto
        - readme         # README content
      max_depth: 5       # Directory tree depth
      include:
        - "**/*.go"
        - "**/*.ts"
      exclude:
        - "**/vendor/**"
        - "**/node_modules/**"

docs.title: Remote Repository
docs.text:
context:
  repositories:
    - url: https://github.com/org/repo.git
      branch: main
      sparse:
        - src/
        - pkg/

Remote repositories are cloned to a cache directory (/tmp/multispec-repos/). Shallow clones are used for performance.

docs.title: Extracted Information
docs.text: Analysis Data Extracted structure Directory tree, file sizes dependencies Package names, versions (go.mod, package.json, requirements.txt, Cargo.toml) api_schemas OpenAPI/Swagger routes, GraphQL queries/mutations, Proto services readme README content (truncated to 5KB)

docs.title: Graphize Integration
docs.text: Graphize provides requirement traceability graphs.

docs.title: Auto-Detection
docs.text:
When graphize: auto is set on a repository, multispec automatically loads graphs from .graphize/ directories.

context:
  repositories:
    - path: /path/to/repo
      graphize: auto  # Detect .graphize/ in repo

docs.title: Standalone Graph
docs.text:
context:
  graphize:
    - path: /path/to/project
      name: requirements
      include_nodes:
        - requirement
        - decision
        - constraint
      include_edges:
        - traces_to
        - depends_on

docs.title: Extracted Information
docs.text: Data Description Nodes Requirements, decisions, constraints, user stories Edges Traceability links between nodes Coverage Percentage of requirements linked to code/tests

docs.title: MCP Servers
docs.text: MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers provide access to external tools.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
context:
  mcp_servers:
    server-name:
      command: npx       # Command to start server
      args: ["-y", "@anthropic/jira-mcp"]
      env:               # Environment variables
        JIRA_URL: https://company.atlassian.net
        JIRA_TOKEN: ${JIRA_TOKEN}
      config:            # Server-specific config
        project: PROJ
        jql: "status != Done ORDER BY updated DESC"
      timeout: 30s

docs.title: Supported Servers
docs.text: Server Package Data Jira @anthropic/jira-mcp Issues, epics, stories Confluence @anthropic/confluence-mcp Pages, spaces Google Docs @anthropic/gdocs-mcp Documents Linear @anthropic/linear-mcp Issues, projects Notion @anthropic/notion-mcp Pages, databases

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Use ${VAR} syntax to reference environment variables:

mcp_servers:
  jira:
    env:
      JIRA_TOKEN: ${JIRA_API_TOKEN}

docs.title: Local Files
docs.text:
Include individual files as context:

context:
  files:
    - path: docs/architecture.md
      type: architecture

    - path: docs/api-spec.yaml
      type: api_spec

    - path: DECISIONS.md
      type: decisions

    - path: diagrams/system.puml
      type: diagram

docs.title: File Types
docs.text: Type Description architecture System architecture documentation api_spec API specifications (OpenAPI, GraphQL) readme README files decisions ADRs, decision logs diagram PlantUML, Mermaid diagrams requirements Requirements documents config Configuration files document Generic documents

docs.title: Gather Context
docs.text:
# Gather from all configured sources
multispec context gather

# Gather with JSON output
multispec context gather --format json

# Force refresh (ignore cache)
multispec context gather --refresh

docs.title: Show Context
docs.text:
# Display context summary
multispec context show

docs.title: Save Snapshot
docs.text:
# Save context to file for CI/reproducibility
multispec context save --output context-snapshot.json

docs.title: List Sources
docs.text:
# Show configured sources
multispec context sources

docs.title: Context in Synthesis
docs.text:
Context is automatically used when synthesizing TRD and IRD:

# TRD synthesis includes context
multispec synthesize trd

# Skip context gathering
multispec synthesize trd --no-context

The context summary is included in the LLM prompt to ground technical decisions.

docs.title: Caching
docs.text:
Context is cached in memory with configurable TTL:

context:
  cache_ttl: 5m  # Default: 5 minutes

Use --refresh to bypass cache:

multispec context gather --refresh

docs.title: Snapshots
docs.text:
Save context snapshots for reproducibility:

# Save snapshot
multispec context save -o context.json

# Use snapshot in CI
multispec synthesize trd --context-file context.json

Snapshots contain all gathered context and can be version controlled.

docs.title: Writing Custom Sources
docs.text:
Implement the Source interface:

package custom

import (
    "context"
    ctx "github.com/plexusone/multispec/pkg/context"
)

type MySource struct {
    // ...
}

func (s *MySource) Name() string {
    return "custom:my-source"
}

func (s *MySource) Type() ctx.SourceType {
    return ctx.SourceTypeFile // or custom type
}

func (s *MySource) Fetch(c context.Context) (*ctx.ContextData, error) {
    // Fetch and return context data
    return &ctx.ContextData{
        Source: s.Name(),
        Type:   s.Type(),
        // ...
    }, nil
}

func (s *MySource) String() string {
    return "My Custom Source"
}

Register with the aggregator:

agg := ctx.NewAggregator("project", cfg)
agg.AddSource(&MySource{})

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Use sparse checkout for large repositories to reduce clone time
  2. Set appropriate TTL - longer for stable codebases, shorter for active development
  3. Use snapshots in CI for reproducible builds
  4. Filter nodes/edges in graphize to reduce context size
  5. Truncate large files - context is automatically truncated for LLM token limits

docs.text: MultiSpec provides a command-line interface for managing specifications.

docs.title: Global Flags
docs.text: Flag Short Description --project -p Project name or path --verbose -v Enable verbose output --help -h Show help --version Show version

docs.title: Project Setup
docs.text: Command Description init Initialize a new project create Create specs from templates lint Validate directory structure status Show project status profiles Manage configuration profiles

docs.title: Spec Workflow
docs.text: Command Description eval Evaluate specs using LLM judges synthesize Generate GTM/technical specs from sources reconcile Generate unified execution spec approve Approve a spec for reconciliation

docs.title: Usage Examples
docs.text:
# Initialize a project
multispec init user-onboarding

# Lint all projects
multispec lint

# Lint specific project
multispec lint user-onboarding

# Check status
multispec status -p user-onboarding

# JSON output
multispec status -p user-onboarding --format json

# Generate HTML report
multispec status -p user-onboarding --format html > status.html

# CI mode (exit non-zero if not ready)
multispec status -p user-onboarding --ci

# List export targets
multispec targets

docs.title: approve
docs.text: Mark a specification as approved for reconciliation.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: multispec approve <spec-type> [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
The approve command marks a specification as approved and ready for reconciliation. Approvals are required before running multispec reconcile to generate the unified execution spec.

Approvals are recorded in multispec.yaml with:

  • Approver identifier
  • Timestamp
  • Optional comment

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Description spec-type Type of spec to approve (mrd, prd, uxd, trd, ird, etc.)

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Type Default Description --approver string $USER Approver email or identifier --comment string "" Approval comment

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Approve PRD with default approver
multispec approve prd

# Approve TRD with specific approver
multispec approve trd --approver=eng@company.com

# Approve with comment
multispec approve mrd --approver=pm@company.com --comment="Reviewed in spec review meeting"

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
✓ Approved prd by john
  Comment: Reviewed in spec review meeting

docs.title: Approval Workflow
docs.text:
1. Write spec - Create or synthesize the specification
  2. Evaluate - Run multispec eval <type> to check quality
  3. Review - Human review of spec content
  4. Approve - Mark as approved with this command
  5. Reconcile - Run multispec reconcile after all approvals

docs.title: Required Approvals
docs.text:
The specs required for reconciliation depend on your profile:

Profile Required Approvals startup prd growth prd, uxd enterprise mrd, prd, uxd, trd

docs.title: Checking Approval Status
docs.text:
Use multispec status to see which specs are approved:

multispec status

# Approvals:
#   ✓ mrd - approved by pm@co on 2024-01-10
#   ✓ prd - approved by pm@co on 2024-01-11
#   ✗ uxd - not approved
#   ✗ trd - not approved

docs.title: Storage
docs.text:
Approvals are stored in multispec.yaml:

approvals:
  mrd:
    approver: pm@company.com
    approved_at: 2024-01-10T14:30:00Z
    comment: "Reviewed in planning meeting"
  prd:
    approver: pm@company.com
    approved_at: 2024-01-11T09:00:00Z

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• eval - Evaluate specs before approval
  • reconcile - Reconcile after all approvals
  • status - Check approval status

docs.title: context
docs.text: Gather and manage codebase context for grounding spec synthesis.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: multispec context <subcommand> [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The context command manages codebase context that grounds technical spec synthesis in reality. Context is gathered from git repositories, graphize requirement graphs, local files, and MCP servers.

docs.title: Subcommands
docs.text: Subcommand Description gather Collect context from all configured sources show Display current context summary save Save context snapshot to file sources List configured context sources

docs.title: context gather
docs.text:
Gather context from all configured sources.

multispec context gather [flags]

Flags:

Flag Type Default Description --timeout duration 2m Timeout for gathering --format string text Output format: text, json --refresh bool false Refresh cache before gathering

Example:

# Gather context
multispec context gather

# Output as JSON
multispec context gather --format json

# Force refresh cached data
multispec context gather --refresh

# Custom timeout
multispec context gather --timeout 5m

docs.title: context show
docs.text:
Display the current context summary from the last gather or saved snapshot.

multispec context show

If no snapshot exists, prompts to run gather first.

docs.title: context save
docs.text:
Save gathered context to a snapshot file for CI reproducibility.

multispec context save [flags]

Flags:

Flag Type Default Description -o, --output string .context-snapshot.json Output file path

Example:

# Save to default location
multispec context save

# Save to custom path
multispec context save -o artifacts/context.json

docs.title: context sources
docs.text:
List all configured context sources.

multispec context sources

Output:

Configured context sources:

Git Repositories:
  - /path/to/repo
    (graphize: auto)

Graphize Graphs:
  - spec-graph (/path/to/.graphize)

Local Files:
  - architecture.md (architecture)
  - docs/adr/*.md (adr)

MCP Servers:
  - jira (not yet implemented)

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configure context sources in multispec.yaml:

context:
  # Git repositories to analyze
  repositories:
    - path: "."
      include_structure: true
      include_deps: true
      include_apis: true
      graphize: auto
    - url: "https://github.com/org/repo"
      branch: main
      shallow: true

  # Graphize requirement graphs
  graphize:
    - path: ".graphize"
      name: "spec-graph"
      include_nodes: true
      include_edges: true

  # Local documentation files
  files:
    - path: "docs/architecture.md"
      type: architecture
    - path: "docs/adr/*.md"
      type: adr
    - path: "api/openapi.yaml"
      type: api_spec

  # MCP servers for external tools
  mcp_servers:
    jira:
      command: "npx"
      args: ["-y", "@anthropic/mcp-jira"]
      tools: ["get_issue", "search_issues"]

  # Cache settings
  cache_ttl: 30m

docs.title: Repository Analysis
docs.text:
When analyzing git repositories, the context includes:

  • Structure - Directory tree and file organization
  • Dependencies - Package dependencies (go.mod, package.json, etc.)
  • APIs - OpenAPI specs, GraphQL schemas, protobuf definitions
  • README - Repository documentation

docs.title: Auto-Detection
docs.text: If no repositories are configured, multispec auto-detects the repository at the project root.

docs.title: Snapshots
docs.text:
Context snapshots enable reproducible synthesis in CI:

# In CI pipeline
multispec context save -o context-snapshot.json

# Later, use saved context
multispec synthesize trd --context-file context-snapshot.json

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Context Sources Guide - Comprehensive configuration guide
  • synthesize - Uses context for TRD/IRD synthesis

docs.title: create
docs.text: Create a new spec file from a template.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: multispec create <spec-type> [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
The create command scaffolds a new specification file from an embedded template. This provides a starting point for writing specs with the correct structure and sections.

The command must be run from within a multispec project directory (where multispec.yaml exists).

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Description spec-type Type of spec to create

docs.title: Supported Spec Types
docs.text: Type Category Description mrd Source Market Requirements Document prd Source Product Requirements Document uxd Source User Experience Design press GTM Press Release (Working Backwards) faq GTM FAQ Document narrative-1p GTM 1-Page Narrative narrative-6p GTM 6-Page Narrative trd Technical Technical Requirements Document ird Technical Infrastructure Requirements Document

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Type Default Description --force bool false Overwrite existing file

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Create a new MRD
multispec create mrd

# Create a PRD
multispec create prd

# Create a Press Release (Working Backwards)
multispec create press

# Overwrite existing TRD
multispec create trd --force

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
The command creates a file in the appropriate directory:

  • Source specs: source/{type}.md
  • GTM specs: gtm/{type}.md
  • Technical specs: technical/{type}.md

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• init - Initialize a new project with --with-templates
  • synthesize - Generate specs from source documents

docs.title: docs
docs.text: Generate MkDocs-compatible documentation files.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: multispec docs <subcommand> [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The docs command generates markdown files for MkDocs integration. It creates index pages for projects and the main specs landing page.

docs.title: Subcommands
docs.text: Subcommand Description generate Generate all index.md files project Generate index.md for a specific project

docs.title: docs generate
docs.text:
Generate index.md files for all projects and the specs landing page.

multispec docs generate [flags]

Flags:

Flag Type Default Description --with-graph bool false Include traceability metrics from spec graph

Output files:

  • docs/specs/index.md - Specs landing page with project listing
  • docs/specs/{project}/index.md - Project index with status and specs table

Example:

# Generate all documentation
multispec docs generate

# Include graph metrics
multispec docs generate --with-graph

docs.title: docs project
docs.text:
Generate index.md for a specific project.

multispec docs project <project-name> [flags]

Flags:

Flag Type Default Description --with-graph bool false Include traceability metrics from spec graph

Example:

# Generate index for specific project
multispec docs project user-onboarding

# With graph metrics
multispec docs project user-onboarding --with-graph

docs.title: Project Index ({project}/index.md)
docs.text:
• Status badge (ready/not ready)
  • Project description
  • Specs table with links
  • Traceability metrics (if --with-graph)

docs.title: Specs Landing (specs/index.md)
docs.text:
• Projects table with status and progress
  • Links to CONSTITUTION.md and ROADMAP.md
  • Cross-project metrics summary

docs.title: Graph Metrics
docs.text:
When using --with-graph, includes:

  • Total requirements count
  • User stories count
  • Trace coverage percentage
  • Conflict count

Requires running multispec graph extract first.

docs.title: MkDocs Integration
docs.text:
The generated files integrate with your MkDocs site. Add to mkdocs.yml:

nav:
  - Specifications:
      - Overview: specs/index.md
      - user-onboarding: specs/user-onboarding/index.md
      - payment-flow: specs/payment-flow/index.md

docs.title: Example Output
docs.text:
specs/index.md:

| Project | Status | Progress |
|---------|--------|----------|
| [user-onboarding](user-onboarding/index.md) | Ready | 100% |
| [payment-flow](payment-flow/index.md) | In Progress | 60% |

## Repository Documents

- [CONSTITUTION.md](CONSTITUTION.md)
- [ROADMAP.md](ROADMAP.md)

docs.text:

  • graph - Extract and query requirement graphs

docs.title: eval
docs.text: Evaluate specifications using LLM-as-a-Judge.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: multispec eval [spec-type] [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
The eval command evaluates specification documents against rubrics using an LLM judge. It checks for quality, completeness, and adherence to best practices.

Evaluation results are saved to eval/{spec-type}.eval.json and include:

  • Overall score (0-10)
  • Pass/fail decision
  • Findings with severity levels
  • Category scores

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Description spec-type Specific spec type to evaluate (optional if using category flags)

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Type Default Description --all bool false Evaluate all specs --source bool false Evaluate source specs only (mrd, prd, uxd) --gtm bool false Evaluate GTM docs only (press, faq, narrative) --technical bool false Evaluate technical docs only (trd, ird)

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Evaluate a specific spec
multispec eval prd

# Evaluate all specs
multispec eval --all

# Evaluate only source specs
multispec eval --source

# Evaluate GTM documents
multispec eval --gtm

# Evaluate technical specs
multispec eval --technical

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
The command prints evaluation status for each spec:

⋯ Evaluating prd...
✓ prd: 8.5/10 PASS (3 findings)

⋯ Evaluating trd...
✗ trd: 5.2/10 FAIL (7 findings)

docs.title: Evaluation Results
docs.text:
Results are saved to eval/{spec-type}.eval.json:

{
  "spec_type": "prd",
  "score": 8.5,
  "passed": true,
  "findings": [
    {
      "category": "user_stories",
      "severity": "medium",
      "message": "User story US-3 missing acceptance criteria"
    }
  ],
  "category_scores": {
    "problem_definition": 9.0,
    "user_stories": 7.5,
    "requirements": 8.5
  }
}

docs.title: Severity Levels
docs.text: Severity Description Impact critical Spec cannot be used Automatic fail high Major issues Likely fail medium Should be addressed May affect score low Minor improvements Informational

docs.title: Pass Criteria
docs.text:
A spec passes evaluation when:

  • Score >= 7.0
  • No critical findings
  • No more than 2 high findings

docs.title: LLM Configuration
docs.text:
The LLM used for evaluation is configured in multispec.yaml:

llm:
  provider: anthropic
  model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
  temperature: 0.3

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• synthesize - Generate specs with optional evaluation
  • approve - Approve specs that pass evaluation

docs.title: export
docs.text: Export the reconciled spec to target execution systems.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: multispec export <target> [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The export command transforms the unified spec.md into formats compatible with AI coding agent execution systems. Each target produces different output files optimized for specific workflows.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before exporting, you must have a reconciled spec.md:

multispec reconcile

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Description target Target system: speckit, gsd, gastown, gascity

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Type Default Description --dry-run bool false Show what would be exported without writing --output string "" Output directory (default: export/<target>)

docs.title: speckit
docs.text:
GitHub Spec-Kit format for spec-driven development.

Output files:

  • spec.md - Unified specification
  • plan.md - Implementation plan with phases
  • tasks.md - Task breakdown

Features:

  • Sequential task execution
  • Constitution sync to .specify/memory/
  • Branch numbering support

docs.title: gsd
docs.text:
Get Shit Done methodology format.

Output files:

  • PLAN.md - Plan with YAML frontmatter (must_haves, truths, artifacts)
  • STATE.md - Progress tracking
  • config.json - GSD configuration

Features:

  • Wave-based parallel execution
  • Artifact verification
  • Progress state tracking

docs.title: gastown
docs.text:
GasTown multi-agent formula format.

Output files:

  • formula.toml - Formula definition (convoy/workflow/expansion)
  • beads/*.toml - Individual task beads

Features:

  • DAG-based execution
  • Bead dependencies
  • Priority ordering

docs.title: gascity
docs.text:
GasCity multi-agent orchestration format.

Output files:

  • city.toml - City configuration with agents and orders

Features:

  • Agent role definitions
  • Order (task) dependencies
  • Orchestration modes (orchestrated, autonomous, hybrid)

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Export to SpecKit
multispec export speckit

# Export to GSD with custom output directory
multispec export gsd --output ./my-gsd-export

# Dry run to see what would be created
multispec export gastown --dry-run

# Export to GasCity
multispec export gascity

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
⋯ Exporting to speckit...
✓ Exported to SpecKit format
  Output: docs/specs/my-project/export/speckit
  Files:
    - spec.md
    - plan.md
    - tasks.md

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Target-specific configuration in multispec.yaml:

targets:
  default: speckit
  speckit:
    enabled: true
    output_dir: export/speckit
    branch_numbering: sequential
  gsd:
    model_profile: balanced
  gastown:
    formula_type: convoy
    rig: my-rig
  gascity:
    city_dir: export/gascity

docs.title: Constitution Sync
docs.text: For SpecKit exports, if CONSTITUTION.md exists, it's synced to .specify/memory/constitution.md for agent memory integration.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• reconcile - Generate spec.md before export
  • targets - List available targets

docs.title: graph
docs.text: Manage requirement graphs using Graphize integration.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The graph command extracts requirement graphs from spec files, enabling traceability analysis and visualization. It integrates with graphize for graph operations.

docs.title: extract
docs.text:
Extract a requirement graph from all specs in the project.

multispec graph extract

Output:

  • Creates .graphize/spec-graph.json in the project directory
  • Prints node count summary by type

Example:

Extracted graph with 45 nodes and 32 edges
Saved to: docs/specs/user-onboarding/.graphize/spec-graph.json

Node types:
  spec: 4
  section: 18
  requirement: 12
  user_story: 6
  constraint: 3
  decision: 2

docs.title: export
docs.text:
Export the graph to various formats for visualization or analysis.

multispec graph export [--format FORMAT] [--output DIR]

Flags:

Flag Default Description --format html Export format: html, graphml, json --output .graphize Output directory

Formats:

Format Output File Description html graph.html Interactive HTML visualization graphml spec-graph.graphml GraphML for graph tools (yEd, Gephi) json spec-graph.json Raw graph data

Examples:

# Export as interactive HTML
multispec graph export --format html

# Export as GraphML for yEd
multispec graph export --format graphml

# Export to custom directory
multispec graph export --format html --output ./reports

docs.title: query
docs.text:
Query nodes in the graph by type or spec.

multispec graph query [--type TYPE] [--spec SPEC]

Flags:

Flag Description --type Filter by node type (requirement, user_story, constraint, decision) --spec Filter by spec type (mrd, prd, uxd, trd)

Examples:

# List all requirements
multispec graph query --type requirement

# List all PRD nodes
multispec graph query --spec prd

# List constraints from TRD
multispec graph query --type constraint --spec trd

Output:

Found 12 nodes

[requirement] The system shall support SSO login
  ID: prd_req_req_prd_1
  Spec: prd
  Text: The system shall support SSO login via SAML 2.0...

[requirement] Users must be able to reset passwords
  ID: prd_req_req_prd_2
  Spec: prd
  Text: Users must be able to reset passwords via email...

docs.title: Node Types
docs.text:
The graph extractor identifies the following node types from specs:

Type Source Pattern requirement PRD Statements with "shall", "must", "should" user_story PRD "As a... I want... so that..." acceptance_criteria PRD "Given/When/Then" patterns constraint MRD, TRD Sections titled "Constraints", "Limitations" decision TRD Sections titled "Decisions", "Architecture" section All Markdown headings spec All Root node for each spec file

docs.title: Edge Types
docs.text:
Relationships extracted between nodes:

Type Description contains Section contains requirements/decisions traces_to Requirement traces to technical decision derived_from TRD derived from PRD and MRD

docs.title: Graph Storage
docs.text:
Graphs are stored in the .graphize/ directory within each project:

docs/specs/{project}/
└── .graphize/
    ├── spec-graph.json      # Primary graph data
    ├── spec-graph.graphml   # GraphML export (if generated)
    └── graph.html           # HTML visualization (if generated)

docs.title: Integration with Graphize
docs.text:
The graph command uses:

  • graphize v0.3.0 - Graph extraction and export
  • graphfs v0.2.0 - Graph data structures

For advanced graph analysis, you can use the graphize CLI directly on the exported JSON:

# Install graphize
go install github.com/plexusone/graphize/cmd/graphize@latest

# Analyze the spec graph
graphize analyze .graphize/spec-graph.json

# Generate detailed report
graphize report .graphize/spec-graph.json

docs.title: multispec init
docs.text: Initialize a new MultiSpec project.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: multispec init <project-name>

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Creates a new project with the standard MultiSpec directory structure:

docs/specs/{project}/
├── source/
│   ├── mrd.md
│   ├── prd.md
│   └── uxd.md
├── gtm/
├── technical/
├── eval/
└── multispec.yaml

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Description project-name Yes Name of the project (kebab-case)

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Create a new project
multispec init user-onboarding

# Create another project
multispec init payment-integration

docs.title: multispec.yaml
docs.text:
Project configuration file:

name: user-onboarding
description: ""
version: 0.1.0

docs.title: Source Spec Placeholders
docs.text:
The source/ directory contains placeholder markdown files:

  • mrd.md - Market Requirements Document
  • prd.md - Product Requirements Document
  • uxd.md - User Experience Design

docs.title: Project Naming
docs.text:
Project names should be:

  • Lowercase
  • Hyphen-separated (kebab-case)
  • Descriptive

Good: user-onboarding, payment-flow, admin-dashboard

Bad: UserOnboarding, payment_flow, ADMIN

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• lint - Validate project structure
  • status - Check project readiness

docs.title: multispec lint
docs.text: Validate project directory structure and naming conventions.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: multispec lint [project] [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Validates that the project follows MultiSpec conventions:

  • Directory structure matches canonical layout
  • File naming follows conventions (lowercase specs, kebab-case projects)
  • Required directories exist
  • Config file is valid

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Required Description project No Project name to lint (lints all if omitted)

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Default Description --format text Output format: text or json --ci false Exit with non-zero code if lint fails

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Lint all projects
multispec lint

# Lint specific project
multispec lint user-onboarding

# JSON output for CI
multispec lint --format json

# CI mode (exit 1 on failure)
multispec lint --ci

docs.title: Directory Structure
docs.text:
The linter checks for the expected directory layout:

docs/specs/{project}/
├── source/       # Required
├── gtm/          # Required
├── technical/    # Required
├── eval/         # Required
└── multispec.yaml  # Required

docs.title: Naming Conventions
docs.text: Element Convention Valid Invalid Project directory kebab-case user-onboarding UserOnboarding Spec files lowercase.md mrd.md MRD.md Eval files lowercase.eval.json mrd.eval.json MRD_eval.json

docs.title: Config File
docs.text:
The multispec.yaml file must exist and be valid YAML with required fields:

name: project-name      # Required
description: "..."      # Optional
version: 0.1.0          # Optional

docs.title: Text Output
docs.text:
Linting project: user-onboarding
  [+] Directory structure valid
  [+] Config file valid
  [+] Naming conventions followed

Result: PASSED

docs.title: JSON Output
docs.text:
{
  "project": "user-onboarding",
  "passed": true,
  "errors": [],
  "warnings": []
}

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Code Description 0 All checks passed 1 Lint failures (with --ci flag)

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• init - Create a new project
  • status - Check project readiness

docs.title: profiles
docs.text: Manage configuration profiles that bundle spec requirements, templates, and rubrics.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: multispec profiles <subcommand>

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Configuration profiles allow you to customize which specs are required and how they're evaluated. MultiSpec includes built-in profiles for different product stages:

Profile Description Required Specs 0-1 Minimal for idea validation hypothesis startup Lightweight for pre-PMF prd growth Metrics-driven for 1-N scaling prd, uxd, faq enterprise Comprehensive for post-PMF prd, mrd, uxd, trd, press, faq

docs.title: list
docs.text:
List all available profiles.

multispec profiles list

Output:

Available profiles:

  0-1          Minimal configuration for idea validation [default]
  startup      Lightweight configuration for pre-PMF startups [default]
  growth       Metrics-driven configuration for 1-N scaling [default]
  enterprise   Comprehensive configuration for post-PMF enterprises [default]

Use with: multispec init <project> --profile <name>

docs.title: show
docs.text:
Show detailed information about a profile.

multispec profiles show <profile-name>

Example:

multispec profiles show enterprise

Output:

Profile: enterprise
Description: Comprehensive configuration for post-PMF enterprises. Full specs with security and compliance.

Required specs:
  - prd (source)
  - mrd (source)
  - uxd (source)
  - press (gtm)
  - faq (gtm)
  - trd (technical)

Custom templates:
  - prd
  - mrd
  - uxd
  - trd
  - press
  - faq

Custom rubrics:
  - prd
  - mrd
  - uxd
  - trd
  - press
  - faq

docs.title: export
docs.text:
Export a profile to a directory for customization.

multispec profiles export <profile-name> <output-dir>

This creates a complete profile directory with:

  • profile.yaml - Configuration file
  • templates/ - Template files (.md)
  • rubrics/ - Rubric files (.rubric.yaml)

Example:

multispec profiles export enterprise ./my-profile

Output:

Created ./my-profile/profile.yaml
Created ./my-profile/templates/prd.md
Created ./my-profile/templates/mrd.md
Created ./my-profile/templates/uxd.md
Created ./my-profile/templates/trd.md
Created ./my-profile/templates/press.md
Created ./my-profile/templates/faq.md
Created ./my-profile/rubrics/prd.rubric.yaml
Created ./my-profile/rubrics/mrd.rubric.yaml
Created ./my-profile/rubrics/uxd.rubric.yaml
Created ./my-profile/rubrics/trd.rubric.yaml
Created ./my-profile/rubrics/press.rubric.yaml
Created ./my-profile/rubrics/faq.rubric.yaml

Profile exported to ./my-profile

To use this profile:
  multispec init my-project --profile-dir ./my-profile

docs.title: With init command
docs.text:
# Use a built-in profile
multispec init my-project --profile startup

# Use a custom profile directory
multispec init my-project --profile-dir ./my-profile

docs.title: Profile inheritance
docs.text:
Profiles can extend other profiles using the extends field:

# my-profile/profile.yaml
name: my-enterprise
description: "Custom enterprise profile with additional requirements"
extends: enterprise

spec_config:
  # Add a custom spec type
  security-review:
    required: true
    category: technical

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Custom Profiles Guide - Complete guide to creating custom profiles
  • init - Initialize projects with profiles

docs.title: reconcile
docs.text: Generate a unified execution spec from approved specifications.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: multispec reconcile [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
The reconcile command combines all approved specifications into a unified execution spec (spec.md). This is the final step before exporting to AI coding agent execution systems.

The reconciliation process:

  1. Verifies all required specs are approved
  2. Detects conflicts between specs
  3. Resolves conflicts using LLM-assisted analysis
  4. Generates unified spec.md
  5. Creates spec.eval.json with reconciliation metadata

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
All required specs must be approved before reconciliation:

multispec approve mrd
multispec approve prd
multispec approve uxd
multispec approve trd

docs.title: Conflict Detection
docs.text:
The reconciler detects conflicts across specs:

Conflict Type Specs Involved Example Performance vs Feature PRD, TRD Feature requires real-time sync but TRD specifies batch processing Security vs Usability UXD, TRD UX flow bypasses security controls Scope vs Timeline MRD, PRD PRD includes features not in MRD scope Missing Traceability PRD, TRD Requirements not covered in technical design

docs.title: Output Files
docs.text:
spec.md - Unified execution specification containing:

  • Executive summary
  • Consolidated requirements
  • Technical architecture
  • Implementation phases
  • Acceptance criteria

eval/spec.eval.json - Reconciliation metadata:

{
  "spec_type": "spec",
  "generated_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "sources": ["mrd", "prd", "uxd", "trd", "ird"],
  "conflicts": [
    {
      "id": "C001",
      "type": "performance_vs_feature",
      "description": "Real-time requirement conflicts with batch architecture",
      "severity": "high",
      "resolution": "Implement hybrid approach with real-time for critical paths"
    }
  ],
  "decision_log": [...],
  "status": "reconciled_with_tradeoffs"
}

docs.title: Reconciliation Status
docs.text: Status Description reconciled No conflicts detected reconciled_with_tradeoffs Conflicts resolved with documented tradeoffs needs_review Unresolved high-severity conflicts

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Run reconciliation
multispec reconcile

# Output:
# ✓ All required specs approved: [mrd prd uxd trd ird]
# ⋯ Reconciling specs...
# ✓ Generated docs/specs/my-project/spec.md
#   Sources: [mrd prd uxd trd ird]
#   Conflicts detected: 2
#     ✓ C001: Performance vs feature trade-off
#     ✓ C002: Security model alignment
# ✓ Generated docs/specs/my-project/eval/spec.eval.json

docs.title: Handling Failures
docs.text:
If reconciliation fails due to missing approvals:

Missing approvals:
  ✗ trd
  ✗ ird

Approve specs with: multispec approve <spec-type>

If high-severity conflicts cannot be resolved, the status will be needs_review and manual intervention is required.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• approve - Approve specs for reconciliation
  • export - Export reconciled spec to target systems
  • status - Check project readiness

docs.title: serve
docs.text: Start the MCP server for AI assistant integration.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: multispec serve [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The serve command starts a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that enables AI coding assistants like Claude Code and Kiro CLI to interact with MultiSpec projects programmatically.

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Type Default Description --port int 0 HTTP port (0 for stdio transport) --transport string stdio Transport mode: stdio, http, sse

docs.title: Transport Modes
docs.text: Mode Use Case stdio Default. Communication via stdin/stdout. Used by Claude Code. http HTTP server on specified port. For custom integrations. sse Server-Sent Events. For browser-based clients.

docs.title: MCP Tools
docs.text: The server exposes these tools to AI assistants:

docs.title: Project Management
docs.text: Tool Description list_projects List all MultiSpec projects get_project_status Get project readiness status

docs.title: Spec Operations
docs.text: Tool Description get_spec Get specification content get_eval Get evaluation results run_eval Run evaluation against rubric synthesize Generate specs from sources reconcile Generate unified spec.md approve Approve a specification export Export to target system

docs.title: Draft Authoring
docs.text: Tool Description start_draft Initialize a new draft get_draft Get current draft content update_draft Save draft content eval_draft Evaluate draft against rubric finalize_draft Promote draft to final spec discard_draft Delete a draft list_drafts List all drafts in project

docs.title: Claude Code Configuration
docs.text:
Add to ~/.claude/claude_desktop_config.json:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "multispec": {
      "command": "multispec",
      "args": ["serve"]
    }
  }
}

Or if using the separate MCP binary:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "multispec": {
      "command": "multispec-mcp"
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Kiro CLI Configuration
docs.text:
Add to your Kiro steering configuration:

mcp:
  servers:
    - name: multispec
      command: multispec
      args: ["serve"]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Start with stdio transport (for Claude Code)
multispec serve

# Start HTTP server on port 8080
multispec serve --port 8080 --transport http

# Start SSE server
multispec serve --port 3000 --transport sse

docs.title: Working Directory
docs.text:
The MCP server operates relative to the current working directory. It discovers projects by looking for:

  1. docs/specs/ directory containing MultiSpec projects
  2. Individual project directories with multispec.yaml

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• MCP Server Overview - Detailed MCP documentation
  • MCP Tools Reference - Complete tool documentation

docs.title: multispec status
docs.text: Show project status and readiness.

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text: multispec status [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Displays the current status of a MultiSpec project, including:

  • Spec existence and status
  • Evaluation results
  • Approval status
  • Readiness gates
  • Overall readiness summary

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Short Default Description --project -p Project name (required) --format text Output format: text, json, html, markdown --ci false Exit non-zero if not ready

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Terminal output
multispec status -p user-onboarding

# JSON for programmatic use
multispec status -p user-onboarding --format json

# HTML report
multispec status -p user-onboarding --format html > status.html

# Markdown for embedding
multispec status -p user-onboarding --format markdown

# CI mode
multispec status -p user-onboarding --ci

docs.title: Text Format
docs.text:
Project: user-onboarding
Path: docs/specs/user-onboarding

Status: NOT READY
Not ready: 2 blockers

Readiness Gates:
  [+] Required specs present: All required specs exist
  [+] Evaluations passing: No blocking eval findings
  [X] Approvals obtained: Pending approvals
  [X] Execution spec generated: spec.md not generated

Specifications:
  TYPE         CATEGORY   EXISTS   EVAL       APPROVED
  ----         --------   ------   ----       --------
  mrd          source     yes      -          -*
  prd          source     yes      -          -*
  uxd          source     yes      -          -
  trd          technical  -        -          -*

  * = required

Summary:
  Total: 10, Present: 3, Evaluated: 0, Approved: 0

docs.title: JSON Format
docs.text:
{
  "project": "user-onboarding",
  "path": "docs/specs/user-onboarding",
  "generated_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "readiness": {
    "ready": false,
    "summary": "Not ready: 2 blockers",
    "gates": [
      {"name": "Required specs present", "passed": true, "message": "All required specs exist"},
      {"name": "Evaluations passing", "passed": true, "message": "No blocking eval findings"},
      {"name": "Approvals obtained", "passed": false, "message": "Pending approvals"},
      {"name": "Execution spec generated", "passed": false, "message": "spec.md not generated"}
    ]
  },
  "specs": [...],
  "summary": {
    "total_specs": 10,
    "present_specs": 3,
    "evaluated_specs": 0,
    "approved_specs": 0
  }
}

docs.title: HTML Format
docs.text:
Generates a self-contained HTML page with:

  • Traffic light status indicator (green/red)
  • Styled readiness gates
  • Spec status table
  • Summary statistics

docs.title: Markdown Format
docs.text:
Generates GitHub-flavored markdown with:

  • Emoji status indicators (:white_check_mark:, :x:)
  • Formatted tables
  • Suitable for embedding in README or docs

docs.title: Readiness Gates
docs.text: Gate Description Required specs present mrd, prd, uxd, trd must exist Evaluations passing No eval files with fail decision Approvals obtained All required specs have approval in config Execution spec generated spec.md file exists

docs.title: Spec Types
docs.text: Type Category Required mrd source Yes prd source Yes uxd source No press gtm No faq gtm No narrative gtm No trd technical Yes ird technical No sec technical No spec reconciled No

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Code Description 0 Command succeeded (or project is ready with --ci) 1 Project not ready (with --ci flag)

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• lint - Validate project structure
  • init - Create a new project

docs.title: synthesize
docs.text: Generate specifications from source documents using LLM synthesis.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: multispec synthesize <type> [flags]

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
The synthesize command generates specification documents from existing source specs using an LLM. It supports two synthesis paths:

GTM Synthesis (Working Backwards)

  • press - Press Release from MRD + PRD
  • faq - FAQ from Press Release
  • narrative-1p - 1-Page Narrative from MRD + PRD
  • narrative-6p - 6-Page Narrative from MRD + PRD + UXD

Technical Synthesis

  • trd - Technical Requirements from MRD + PRD + UXD + CONSTITUTION + CONTEXT
  • ird - Infrastructure Requirements from TRD + CONSTITUTION + CONTEXT

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text: Argument Description type Spec type to synthesize: trd, ird, press, faq, narrative-1p, narrative-6p

docs.title: Flags
docs.text: Flag Type Default Description --eval bool false Run evaluation after synthesis --no-context bool false Skip context gathering for technical synthesis

docs.title: Required Sources
docs.text: Target Required Sources press MRD, PRD faq Press Release narrative-1p MRD, PRD narrative-6p MRD, PRD, UXD trd MRD, PRD, UXD ird TRD

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Generate Press Release
multispec synthesize press

# Generate TRD with evaluation
multispec synthesize trd --eval

# Generate IRD without context gathering
multispec synthesize ird --no-context

# Generate FAQ document
multispec synthesize faq

docs.title: Context Grounding
docs.text:
For TRD and IRD synthesis, the command automatically gathers codebase context if configured in multispec.yaml:

context:
  repositories:
    - path: "."
      include_structure: true
      include_deps: true
      include_apis: true

This grounds technical decisions in the reality of existing code. Use --no-context to skip this step.

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
⋯ Gathering codebase context for grounding...
  Gathered context from 2 sources
⋯ Synthesizing trd from [mrd prd uxd]...
✓ Generated docs/specs/my-project/technical/trd.md

⋯ Evaluating trd...
✓ trd: 8.2/10 PASS

docs.title: LLM Configuration
docs.text:
Configure the LLM in multispec.yaml:

llm:
  provider: anthropic
  model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
  temperature: 0.7
  max_tokens: 8192

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• eval - Evaluate synthesized specs
  • reconcile - Combine specs into execution spec
  • context - Manage context sources

docs.title: targets
docs.text: List available export targets and their capabilities.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: multispec targets

docs.title: Description
docs.text: The targets command displays all available export targets that can be used with multispec export.

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
Available targets:

  speckit   GitHub Spec-Kit format (spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md)
  gsd       Get Shit Done format (PLAN.md, STATE.md)
  gastown   GasTown formulas and beads

docs.title: Target Comparison
docs.text: Feature SpecKit GSD GasTown GasCity Sequential tasks Yes Yes Yes Yes Parallel execution No Yes (waves) Yes (convoy) Yes Multi-agent No No Yes Yes Verification Implicit Yes Yes Yes Dependency graph Yes Yes Yes (Beads) Yes

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Guidelines for contributing to MultiSpec.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Go 1.21 or later
  • Make
  • golangci-lint (for linting)

docs.title: Clone and Build
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/plexusone/multispec.git
cd multispec
make build

docs.title: Run Tests
docs.text: make test

docs.title: Run Linter
docs.text: make lint

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
multispec/
├── cmd/
│   ├── multispec/      # Main CLI entry point
│   └── mcp-server/     # MCP server entry point
├── internal/
│   ├── cli/            # CLI commands (Cobra)
│   └── mcp/            # MCP server implementation
├── pkg/
│   ├── config/         # Configuration loading
│   ├── lint/           # Linting logic
│   ├── status/         # Status generation
│   └── types/          # Core types
├── docs/               # MkDocs documentation
└── Makefile

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
• Use gofmt for formatting
  • Follow standard Go conventions
  • Run golangci-lint run before committing
  • Keep functions focused and small

docs.title: Commit Messages
docs.text:
Follow Conventional Commits:

feat: add new feature
fix: resolve bug
docs: update documentation
refactor: restructure code
test: add tests
chore: maintenance tasks

docs.title: Pull Requests
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository
  2. Create a feature branch
  3. Make your changes
  4. Run tests and linter
  5. Submit a pull request

docs.title: Adding a New Command
docs.text:
1. Create a new file in internal/cli/
  2. Define the Cobra command
  3. Register in internal/cli/root.go
  4. Add tests
  5. Update documentation in docs/cli/

docs.title: Adding an MCP Tool
docs.text:
1. Define argument types in internal/mcp/server.go
  2. Add the tool registration in registerTools()
  3. Implement the handler function
  4. Add documentation in docs/mcp/tools.md

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
Documentation is built with MkDocs. To preview locally:

pip install mkdocs-material
mkdocs serve

docs.title: Roadmap
docs.text: See ROADMAP.md for planned features and implementation status.

docs.title: Specification Types
docs.text: MultiSpec organizes specifications into three categories:

docs.title: Source Specs (Human-Authored)
docs.text: Spec Name Purpose MRD Market Requirements Market problem, audience, business goals PRD Product Requirements User stories, functional requirements UXD User Experience Design User journeys, interaction flows

docs.title: GTM Specs (LLM-Generated)
docs.text:
Generated using the Working Backwards methodology:

Spec Name Input Purpose PRESS Press Release MRD + PRD Customer announcement format FAQ FAQ Document PRESS Challenges claims, surfaces gaps NARRATIVE Narrative MRD + PRD + FAQ Internal vision document

docs.title: Technical Specs (LLM-Generated)
docs.text: Spec Name Input Purpose TRD Technical Requirements MRD + PRD + UXD + CONSTITUTION Architecture, APIs, data models IRD Infrastructure Requirements TRD + CONSTITUTION Deployment, scaling, operations

docs.title: Directory Structure
docs.text:
docs/specs/
├── CONSTITUTION.md           # Repo-level governance (required)
├── ROADMAP.md                # Cross-project priorities
└── {project}/                # kebab-case project name
    ├── source/               # Human-authored
    │   ├── mrd.md
    │   ├── prd.md
    │   └── uxd.md
    ├── gtm/                  # LLM-generated GTM
    │   ├── press.md
    │   ├── faq.md
    │   └── narrative.md
    ├── technical/            # LLM-generated technical
    │   ├── trd.md
    │   └── ird.md
    ├── eval/                 # Evaluation results
    │   ├── mrd.eval.json
    │   ├── prd.eval.json
    │   └── ...
    ├── spec.md               # Reconciled execution spec
    ├── current-truth.md      # Post-ship maintained state
    └── multispec.yaml        # Project configuration

docs.title: Configuration Profiles
docs.text:
Profiles bundle spec requirements, templates, and rubrics for different use cases:

Profile Use Case Required Specs 0-1 Idea validation hypothesis startup Pre-PMF startups prd growth 1-N scaling prd, uxd, faq enterprise Post-PMF enterprises prd, mrd, uxd, trd, press, faq 
# Use a profile when initializing
multispec init my-project --profile startup

# List available profiles
multispec profiles list

# Export a profile for customization
multispec profiles export enterprise ./my-profile

Organizations can create custom profiles with their own templates and rubrics. See the Custom Profiles Guide for details.

docs.title: Readiness Gates
docs.text:
Projects progress through readiness gates:

  1. Required specs present - All required source specs exist
  2. Evaluations passing - No critical/high findings
  3. Approvals obtained - Required specs have approvals
  4. Execution spec generated - spec.md created via reconciliation

docs.title: Evaluation System
docs.text:
Each spec can be evaluated using LLM-as-a-Judge:

  • Rubrics define evaluation criteria per spec type
  • Findings are categorized by severity (critical, high, medium, low)
  • Decisions are pass, conditional, or fail

Evaluation results are stored as {spec}.eval.json in the eval/ directory.

docs.title: Reconciliation
docs.text:
Reconciliation combines all approved specs into a unified execution spec:

  1. Loads all approved source, GTM, and technical specs
  2. Detects conflicts and missing traceability
  3. Generates spec.md (unified execution spec)
  4. Generates spec.eval.json (reconciliation evaluation)

docs.title: Target Adapters
docs.text:
The reconciled spec.md can be exported to various execution systems:

Target Format Use Case SpecKit spec.md, plan.md, tasks.md GitHub-based workflows GSD PLAN.md, STATE.md Get Shit Done methodology GasTown TOML formulas, Beads Multi-agent orchestration GasCity city.toml Agent city configuration OpenSpec JSON/YAML Portable interchange format

docs.title: CONSTITUTION.md
docs.text:
The CONSTITUTION.md file at docs/specs/CONSTITUTION.md defines repo-level constraints and patterns:

  • Technology choices
  • Coding standards
  • Architecture patterns
  • Security requirements
  • Performance targets

All generated specs must adhere to the constitution.

docs.title: Requirement Graphs
docs.text:
MultiSpec extracts requirement graphs from specs for traceability analysis:

  • Nodes: requirements, user stories, constraints, decisions, sections
  • Edges: traces_to, derived_from, contains

Graphs enable visualization and traceability analysis:

# Extract graph from specs
multispec graph extract

# Export as interactive HTML
multispec graph export --format html

# Query specific node types
multispec graph query --type requirement --spec prd

See Graph Command for full documentation.

docs.title: Post-Ship Alignment
docs.text:
After shipping, current-truth.md maintains alignment between specs and reality:

  • Documents actual capabilities
  • Notes divergences from spec
  • Tracks limitations discovered in production
  • Updates GTM docs with alignment notes

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: • Go 1.21 or later

docs.title: Install via go install
docs.text:
go install github.com/plexusone/multispec/cmd/multispec@latest

This installs the multispec CLI to your $GOPATH/bin directory.

docs.title: Build from Source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/plexusone/multispec.git
cd multispec
make build

The binaries will be placed in the bin/ directory:

  • bin/multispec - Main CLI
  • bin/multispec-mcp - MCP server

docs.title: Install Locally
docs.text:
make install

This copies the binaries to $GOPATH/bin.

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
multispec --version
multispec --help

docs.title: MCP Server
docs.text:
The MCP server is a separate binary for integration with AI assistants:

# Install both binaries
go install github.com/plexusone/multispec/cmd/multispec@latest
go install github.com/plexusone/multispec/cmd/multispec-mcp@latest

See MCP Server for configuration details.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: This guide walks you through creating your first MultiSpec project.

docs.title: Initialize a Project
docs.text:
Create a new project with the standard directory structure:

multispec init user-onboarding

This creates:

docs/specs/user-onboarding/
├── source/
│   ├── mrd.md      # Market Requirements (placeholder)
│   ├── prd.md      # Product Requirements (placeholder)
│   └── uxd.md      # User Experience Design (placeholder)
├── gtm/            # For LLM-generated GTM docs
├── technical/      # For LLM-generated technical docs
├── eval/           # For evaluation results
└── multispec.yaml  # Project configuration

docs.title: Author Source Specs
docs.text: Edit the source specifications in the source/ directory:

docs.title: source/mrd.md - Market Requirements
docs.text:
# Market Requirements Document

## Problem Statement

What problem does this solve?

## Target Audience

Who benefits from this?

## Business Goals

What business metrics will improve?

docs.title: source/prd.md - Product Requirements
docs.text:
# Product Requirements Document

## User Stories

- As a user, I want to...

## Functional Requirements

1. The system shall...

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Criterion 1
- [ ] Criterion 2

docs.title: source/uxd.md - User Experience Design
docs.text:
# User Experience Design

## User Journey

1. User opens app
2. User sees...

## Interaction Flows

Describe key interactions...

docs.title: Validate Your Project
docs.text:
Check that your project follows MultiSpec conventions:

multispec lint user-onboarding

docs.title: Check Project Status
docs.text:
View the current status and readiness:

# Terminal output
multispec status -p user-onboarding

# JSON format
multispec status -p user-onboarding --format json

# Generate HTML report
multispec status -p user-onboarding --format html > status.html

docs.title: Readiness Gates
docs.text:
The status command shows readiness gates:

Gate Requirement Required specs present mrd.md, prd.md, uxd.md, trd.md exist Evaluations passing No critical/high findings in evals Approvals obtained Required specs have approvals in multispec.yaml Execution spec generated spec.md exists

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
Once your source specs are complete:

  1. Synthesize GTM docs 

    multispec synthesize press
multispec synthesize faq
multispec synthesize narrative

  2. Synthesize technical docs 

    multispec synthesize trd
multispec synthesize ird

  3. Run evaluations 

    multispec eval --all

  4. Get approvals 

    multispec approve prd
multispec approve trd

  5. Reconcile to execution spec 

    multispec reconcile

  6. Export to target system 

    multispec export speckit

  7. Extract and visualize requirement graph 

    multispec graph extract
multispec graph export --format html
multispec graph query --type requirement
